titleStmt.title: SII 9.624: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 624. (A.R. No. 384 of 1920.) ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE WELL AT KASAVAPURAM, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 1469, Plavaṅga, Bhādrapada, śu. 15, Maṁ[gaḷavāra], corre sponding to A.D. 1547 August 30, Tuesday, in the reign of Sadāśivadēva-Mahārāya It records that at the request of the sons of Puli Appana-Bōva of Kasavāpura, which had been granted by Kōnēṭi-Timmarājayya, son of Rāmarāja-Koṇḍarāja, as an umbaḷi to Daḷavāyi Yallappa-Nāyaka, the latter abolished biṭṭi, birāḍa and other cesses levied from the fishermen. The capture of cocks and pigs was also prohibited. The gift was made for the merit of Kōnēṭi-Timmarājayya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . svasti śrīja
yābhyudayaśālivā
hanaśakavaruṣaṁgaḷu 1469 ne
ya plavaṁgasaṁvatsarada bhādrapada
śu 15 maṁ lū sadāśivadēvamahā
rāyaru pruthvīrājyaṁgaivuttiralu
rāmarājakoṁḍarājagaḷa ku
mara kōnēṭi tiṁmarājayyanava
ru taṁma daḷavāyi yallappanā
(nā)yakarige Uṁbaḷiyāgi koṭṭa ka
savāpuradalli yiha besta bō
vugaḷige puli Appanabōvana ma
kaḷu gādanabōva parusanabōvu ga
ḷu konēṭi timmarājayyage puṇya
vāgabēkeṁdu daḷavāyi yalla
ppanāyakarige biṁnaha māḍi
terige horige kraya sidhāya bi
ṭi birāḍagaḷa biḍisadaru kōḷi
haṁdigaḷa hiḍiya bāradu I
dakke Āru tappidare taṁma tāya
tāvu suṁdidavaru katteya
tuṁṇe tiṁdavaru sure ku
ḍidavaru śrī śrī śrī .
Digital edition of SII 9.624 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
